meaning: n.呆瓜vt.弄糟vi.混,打发时间,出大错
examples: But was it, in fact, a hideous goof?
Don't goof up. This project is too important.
After dinner is when Obama gets to "goof off a little bit - play with Bo," the new dog.
When Web surfers aren't cruising for cheesecake, they're still looking to goof off.
Setting aside time to goof off at work can relieve stress, improve morale and even help with team building efforts.
